zh.Tillington, Burghill, Herefordshire, Eng: 英國英格蘭賀福爾波吉爾
zh.Tillington, Gloucestershire, England, Un: 英國英格蘭格盧斯特斯爾
zh.lifeSummary: 當Isabel de la Barre於大約1337年出生在英國賀福爾時，她的父親Walter de la Barre68歲，母親Sybil Harley38歲。她於大約1354年與John d'Eylesford of Tillington在英國賀福爾提林頓結婚。他們至少是1個兒子的父母。她去世於1421年9月17日在英國賀福爾提林頓享年85歲。

zh-hans.Tillington, Burghill, Herefordshire, Eng: 英国英格兰賀福爾波吉爾
zh-hans.Tillington, Gloucestershire, England, Un: 英国英格兰格盧斯特斯爾
zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Isabel de la Barre于大约1337年出生在英国賀福爾时，她的父亲Walter de la Barre68岁，母亲Sybil Harley38岁。她于大约1354年与John d'Eylesford of Tillington在英国賀福爾提林頓结婚。他们至少是1个儿子的父母。她去世于1421年9月17日在英国賀福爾提林頓享年85岁。

nameMeanings.name: de
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Dilip, Anirban, Gopal, Alok, Anindya, Arunava, Asit, Asok, Dibyendu, Goutam, Indra, Indranil.
Indian: variant of   Dey  .

variants.name: Dey
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Ashit, Indu, Naina, Partha, Soumya, Ajit, Anjan, Anjana, Anup, Arjun, Arun, Ashim.
English, Scottish, and Welsh: variant of   Day  .
German: topographic name from a field name in North Rhine-Westphalia, denoting a sizeable piece of land, from Middle Low German dē ‘thigh’ (referring to the shape of the property).

nameMeanings.name: la
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Vietnamese Hung, Cuong, Anh, Thanh, Ha, Hoang, Tuan, Chau, Hao, Hien, Hoa, Hue, Nam, Phong, Sinh, Tuong, Min, Tam, Tanh, Thai, Tham, Tinh, Yeong.  Chinese Hong, Hang, Sang, Tong, Chan, Chi, Hwa Ja, Hyung, Kam, Ki Bong, Kin, Man.
Vietnamese: from the Chinese surname 羅, see   Luo   1.
Vietnamese (Lã): from the Chinese surname 呂, see Lu 6 and 3 below.

variants.name: Luo
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Chinese Wei, Jian, Yi, Ping, Yong, Zhen, Feng, Hong, Ming, Jin, Bin, Chen, Hai, Lan, Min, Huan, Hung, Shen, Tian.
Chinese:

Mandarin form of the surname 羅, meaning ‘net for catching birds’ or ‘to collect’ in Chinese: (i) from Luo (羅), the name of an ancient state (located in present-day Hubei province). After it was annexed by the state of Chu during the early Spring and Autumn period (770–476  BC ), people from the state migrated southwards and adopted Luo (羅) as their surname. (ii) from the Po Duo Luo (破多羅) and Chi Luo (叱羅) families from the Xianbei ethnic group in northern China, who changed their original surnames to 羅 during the Northern Wei dynasty (386–534  AD ). (iii) from the personal name Hu Se Luo (斛瑟羅), leader of an ancient Turkic tribe who are believed to have migrated to China during the Tang dynasty (618–907  AD ). His descendants inherited his name Hu Se Luo (斛瑟羅) as their surname and shortened it to 羅 during the Northern Wei dynasty (386–534  AD ). (iv) from the name of one of the seven families from the Bandun Man, an ethnic group residing in ancient southwestern China (located mainly in present-day Sichuan province).

nameMeanings.name: Barre
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French Anatole, Emile, Pierre, Alphonse, Andre, Cecile, Henri, Herve, Jacques, Marcel, Regean, Yves.

French: topographic name for someone who lived by a gateway or barrier, or in a house encircled by a fence, from Old French barre ‘bar, obstruction’ (of obscure origin, possibly akin to the Celtic element barr ‘height’); or a habitational name from any of various places called with this word, e.g. Barre-en-Ouche in Eure or Barre-de-Semilly in Manche. Compare Labarre .
French: probably also a habitational name from Barre in Lozère, named with the Celtic element barr ‘height’.

variants.name: Labarre
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French Pierre, Alain, Andre, Armand, Aurore, Fernande, Gilles, Herve, Laure, Michel, Urbain.

French: topographic name for someone who lived by a gateway or barrier, or in a house encircled by a fence, from Old French barre ‘bar, obstruction’ (see Barre 1), with fused feminine definite article la; or a habitational name from any of various places called with this word, e.g. Barre-en-Ouche in Eure or Barre-de-Semilly in Manche. Compare Labar , Labare , Labarr , Labor , Labore , Lebar , and Lebarre .
History: In North America, this surname was originally also a secondary surname or dit (‘also called’) name, borne by Jacques Genest or Genet dit Labarre from France (see Genest ).

variants.name: Baray
meanings: Hispanic (Mexico): unexplained.
In some cases possibly also French: variant of Barray, itself a variant of Barrey, a derivative of Barre 1. In France, it is found mainly in Seine-Maritime.

variants.name: Barrere
meanings: French (Barrère): topographic name for someone who lived by a gate or barrier, or a metonymic occupational name for a gatekeeper, from a derivative of Old French bar(r)e ‘bar, rod’, in the sense of ‘barrier’ (compare Barre 1); or a habitational name from (La) Barrère, the name of several places in the southwestern part of France.
English: variant of Barrer, for someone who lived by a town or castle gate, from Middle English barre ‘barrier, gateway’. Compare above and Barr 4.

variants.name: Barriere
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French Emile, Gaston, Monique, Philippe.

French (Barrière): topographic name for someone who lived by a gate or barrier, or a metonymic occupational name for a gatekeeper, from a derivative of Old French bar(r)e ‘bar, rod’, in the sense of ‘barrier’ (compare Barre 1); or a habitational name from (La) Barrière, the name of several places in various parts of France. Compare Gates .

variants.name: Barreau
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French/Haitian Fresnel, Gabrielle, Jean-Louis, Jean-Marie, Jeanty, Michel, Michelene, Pierre.
French:

derivative of Old French bar(r)e ‘bar, rod’, in the sense of ‘barrier’ (compare Barre 1), hence a nickname for a fence keeper or a topographic name for someone who lived in a house encircled by a fence.

variants.name: Barrier
meanings: French: occupational name for a gatekeeper, from Old French barier, an agent derivative of bar(r)e ‘bar, rod’, in the sense of ‘barrier’ (compare Barre 1).
Altered form of French   Barriere  .
Altered form of German   Berger  .

variants.name: Barra
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Italian Angelo, Marina, Antonio, Augusto, Biagio, Carlo, Domenic, Flavio, Gennaro, Lorenzo, Luciano, Rocco, Salvatore.  Spanish Jorge, Carlos, Diego, Enrique, Jaime, Juan, Manuel.
Italian (southern), Spanish, and Portuguese: topographic name from barra ‘bar, rod’ (in the sense of ‘barrier, sandbank’), or a habitational name from any of the places called with this word.
French (southern): dialect variant of   Barre   1.

nameMeanings.name: Isabel
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Originally a Spanish version of   Elizabeth  , which was coined by deletion of the first syllable and alteration of the final consonant sound to one that can normally end a word in Spanish. The name was imported into France in the early Middle Ages, and thence into England. It was a royal name, and its enduring popularity may have been enhanced by the fact that it was borne by a queen of England—  Isabella  (  1296–1358 ), daughter of  Philip IV  of France—even though she led a turbulent life and eventually had her husband,  Edward II , murdered.

variants.name: Isabell
meanings: Originally a Spanish version of   Elizabeth  , which was coined by deletion of the first syllable and alteration of the final consonant sound to one that can normally end a word in Spanish. The name was imported into France in the early Middle Ages, and thence into England. It was a royal name, and its enduring popularity may have been enhanced by the fact that it was borne by a queen of England—  Isabella  (  1296–1358 ), daughter of  Philip IV  of France—even though she led a turbulent life and eventually had her husband,  Edward II , murdered.

variants.name: Isabelle
meanings: form of   Isabel  , now in frequent use in the English-speaking world.

variants.name: Isobella
meanings: form of   Isabel  , which has been popular in England since the 18th century and has recently become the preferred form of the name.

variants.name: Isobelle
meanings: Originally a Spanish version of   Elizabeth  , which was coined by deletion of the first syllable and alteration of the final consonant sound to one that can normally end a word in Spanish. The name was imported into France in the early Middle Ages, and thence into England. It was a royal name, and its enduring popularity may have been enhanced by the fact that it was borne by a queen of England—  Isabella  (  1296–1358 ), daughter of  Philip IV  of France—even though she led a turbulent life and eventually had her husband,  Edward II , murdered.

variants.name: Isobel
meanings: Variant spelling of   Isabel .

historicEvents.title: The Hundred Years' War
historicEvents.description: The Hundred Years' War between France and England was largely due to the question of legitimate succession to the French crown.

historicEvents.title: Wycliffe's Bible
historicEvents.description: John Wycliffe, English theologian and professor at the University of Oxford, completed a translation of the Bible from the Latin Vulgate into Middle English. This translation is known as Wycliffe's Bible and was completed around 1382.

en.lifeSummary: When Isabel de la Barre was born about 1337, in Herefordshire, England, her father, Walter de la Barre, was 68 and her mother, Sybil Harley, was 38. She married John d'Eylesford of Tillington about 1354, in Tillington, Herefordshire, England. They were the parents of at least 1 son. She died on 17 September 1421, in Tillington, Herefordshire, England, at the age of 85.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Isabel de la Barre est née environ 1337, à Herefordshire, Angleterre, son père, Walter de la Barre, avait 68 ans et sa mère, Sybil Harley, avait 38 ans. Elle a épousé John d'Eylesford of Tillington environ 1354, à Tillington, Herefordshire, Angleterre. Ils ont eu au moins 1 fils. Elle est décédée le 17 septembre 1421, à Tillington, Herefordshire, Angleterre, à l’âge de 85 ans.

de.lifeSummary: Als Isabel de la Barre um 1337 in Herefordshire, England geboren wurde, war ihr Vater Walter de la Barre 68 und ihre Mutter Sybil Harley 38. Sie hat um 1354 in Tillington, Herefordshire, England John d'Eylesford of Tillington geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 1 Sohn. Sie starb am 17. September 1421 im Alter von 85 Jahren in Tillington, Herefordshire, England.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Isabel de la Barre è nata il circa 1337 nel Herefordshire, England, sua padre, Walter de la Barre, aveva 68 anni e sua madre, Sybil Harley, aveva 38 anni. Lei sposato John d'Eylesford of Tillington circa 1354, nel Tillington, Herefordshire, England. Erano genitori di almeno 1 figlio. Morì il 17 settembre 1421, nel Tillington, Herefordshire, England, all'età di 85 anni.

ja.lifeSummary: Isabel de la Barreが約1337年にイギリスハーエフォードシュアイアで生まれた時，父Walter de la Barreは68歳，母Sybil Harleyは38歳であった。約1354年にイギリスハーエフォードシュアイアティリントンでJohn d'Eylesford of Tillingtonと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも1人の息子の親であった。彼女は1421年9月17日にイギリスハーエフォードシュアイアティリントンで85歳で死去した。

ko.lifeSummary: Isabel de la Barre(이)가 대략 1337년 영국 허퍼드실르에서 태어났을 때, 그녀의 아버지 Walter de la Barre(은)는 68세였고, 그녀의 어머니 Sybil Harley(은)는 38세였다. 그녀는 대략 1354년 영국 허퍼드실르 틸링턴에서 John d'Eylesford of Tillington(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 한 명의 아들을 두었다. 그녀는 1421년 9월 17일 영국 허퍼드실르 틸링턴에서 85세의 나이로 사망했다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Isabel de la Barre nasceu aproximadamente 1337, em Herefordshire, Inglaterra, seu pai, Walter de la Barre, tinha 68 anos e sua mãe, Sybil Harley, tinha 38 anos. Ela casou-se com John d'Eylesford of Tillington aproximadamente 1354, em Tillington, Herefordshire, Inglaterra. Eles tiveram pelo menos 1 filho. Ela faleceu em 17 de setembro de 1421, em Tillington, Herefordshire, Inglaterra, com 85 anos.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Isabel de la Barre родилась около 1337 г. в местности хирефордшир, Англия, её отцу по имени Walter de la Barre было 68 лет, а матери, которую звали Sybil Harley, 38. Она вышла замуж за человека по имени John d'Eylesford of Tillington около 1354 г. в местности тиллингтон, хирефордшир, Англия. У них был по крайней мере 1 сын. Она умерла 17 сентября 1421 г. в местности тиллингтон, хирефордшир, Англия в возрасте 85 лет.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Isabel de la Barre nació aproximadamente 1337, en Herefordshire, Inglaterra, su papá, Walter de la Barre, tenía 68 años y su mamá, Sybil Harley, tenía 38 años. Se casó con John d'Eylesford of Tillington aproximadamente 1354, en Tillington, Herefordshire, Inglaterra. Fueron padres de por lo menos 1 hijo. Murió el 17 de septiembre de 1421, en Tillington, Herefordshire, Inglaterra, a la edad de 85 años.
